transcript.speaker: Moderator
transcript.text: Thanks for making the time. To start, tell me about your work.

transcript.speaker: P5
transcript.text: I'm a researcher on a mid-market team.

transcript.speaker: Moderator
transcript.text: How did pricing factor into your decision?

transcript.speaker: P5
transcript.text: The pricing was opaque — I genuinely could not forecast the cost for next quarter.

transcript.speaker: Moderator
transcript.text: How do you find past research in the tool?

transcript.speaker: P5
transcript.text: Finding an old highlight returns too many irrelevant results.

transcript.speaker: Moderator
transcript.text: How was it for new people picking up the tool?

transcript.speaker: P5
transcript.text: It is powerful but complex — new researchers need more training and docs to ramp.

transcript.speaker: Moderator
transcript.text: That’s really helpful — anything else before we wrap?

transcript.speaker: P5
transcript.text: No, I think that covers the main pain points.

themes.label: Pricing opacity erodes trust before the first invoice
themes.summary: Buyers cannot predict what the product will cost next quarter, so they stop advocating for it internally with their manager and finance.

themes.label: Search returns noisy results
themes.summary: Finding the right past study or highlight returns noisy results that need manual filtering by tag and date.

themes.label: Powerful but steep to learn
themes.summary: New researchers find the tool powerful but complex, and want better training and documentation to ramp up.

quotes.claim: The onboarding was impossible and we almost churned in week one.
